data.title: The9-backed Nanyang Biologics to go public via $1.5B SPAC merger; shares up
data.description:
• Nanyang Biologics, backed by The9 Limited (NASDAQ:NCTY), will merge with SPAC RF Acquisition Corp II (NASDAQ:RFAI) to become publicly listed on Nasdaq.
  • The combined company will trade under the reserved ticker symbol “NYB” after closing.
  • The deal values NYB at a $1.5B pre-transaction equity value.
  • NYB shareholders, including The9, will roll over 100% of their equity and retain majority ownership and board control.
  • The transaction has board approval and is expected to close in Q1 or Q2 2026.
  • NYB will leverage its AI platform to speed up drug discovery and develop natural ingredient-based therapies.
  • NCTY shares up 13% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Popeyes expanding across Mexico with pipeline of 300+ new restaurants
data.description:
• Restaurant Brands International’s (NYSE:QSR) Popeyes chicken franchise is planning a rapid expansion across Mexico by partnering with regional franchisees, including long-standing Popeyes developer, Star Louisiana.
  • The company plans to open more than 300 Popeyes restaurants over the next 10 years across the Northwest, West, Central, and Southeast regions of Mexico. 
  • “Mexico represents a tremendous growth opportunity for Popeyes, and we're thrilled to be partnering with experienced and passionate franchisees who share our vision," said Restaurant Brands’ president Duncan Montero.  
  • By expanding across Mexico, Restaurant Brands (NYSE:QSR) aims to capitalize on one of the company’s fastest growing demographics, and Popeyes’ international growth momentum.
  • Besides Popeyes, Restaurant Brands (NYSE:QSR) is also the parent company of Burger King, Firehouse Subs, and Tim Hortons.

data.title: HP unveils products and services for workspaces, AI development
data.description:
HP (NYSE:HPQ) announced 14 new products and services to enhance workspaces, AI workflows, remote device support, and smart printing experiences.

For personalized workspaces, HP introduced displays and a dock that features proximity activation. The products include, HP Series 5 Pro 14” Portable Monitor, HP Series 5 Pro 49” Conferencing Monitor and HP Ultra-Fast Scroll Wireless Mouse 785M.

The HP USB-C 100W G6 Dock features HP Quick Connect, which uses Bluetooth to detect a PC as it’s approaching and wakes paired technology to start work immediately, according to the company.

For AI development, the company unveiled the HP ZGX Nano G1n AI Station and HP ZGX Toolkit. The AI Station features Nvidia's GB10 Grace Blackwell Superchip.

The company also announced new features for IT, like Remote Connect, to securely access employee devices, perform live troubleshooting, and remediate issues using HP Workforce Experience Platform.

For printing, HP introduced AI-powered features for smarter, sustainable workflows, alongside the new HP DesignJet T870 and HP Envy Photo series.

data.title: S&P Global appoints new CEO of S&P Dow Jones Indices
data.description:
• Catherine Clay will become CEO of S&P (NYSE:SPGI) Dow Jones Indices effective November 1, 2025.
  • Clay succeeds Dan Draper, who will remain as Special Advisor for a transition period.
  • Clay also joins the S&P DJI Board, replacing Draper in that role.
  • Clay was previously EVP and Global Head of Derivatives at Cboe Global Markets.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Blue Ant Media acquires MagellanTV for $12M
data.description:
• Blue Ant Media (TSX:BAMI:CA) acquired MagellanTV LLC for $12M USD.
  • The deal strengthens Blue Ant’s position in premium factual content and global streaming.
  • MagellanTV offers SVOD, AVOD, and FAST channels and reaches audiences in 13 countries via 20+ platforms including Roku, Amazon, and Samsung.
  • Co-founder Greg Diefenbach joins Blue Ant as EVP, Streaming; Thomas Lucas will support the transition.
  • The acquisition enhances Blue Ant’s monetization, distribution, and audience expansion strategies.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: GFL Environmental declares $0.0154 dividend
data.description:
• GFL Environmental (NYSE:GFL) declares $0.0154/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 0.13%
  • Payable Oct. 31; for shareholders of record Oct. 15; ex-div Oct. 15.
  • See GFL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Williams to spend $3.1B on two power projects
data.description:
Williams Companies (NYSE:WMB) disclosed late Wednesday that it plans to spend ~$3.1 billion on two projects to supply power to data centers, in an effort to deliver speed-to-market solutions in grid-constrained markets.

Williams (NYSE:WMB) said the projects, which are anticipated to be completed in H1 2027, are backed by 10-year, primarily fixed-price power purchase agreements with an unnamed large, investment-grade counterparty with an option for the customer to extend; it did not disclose the locations of the two projects in the filing.

The company said the agreements take the total capital committed to such "power innovation" projects to ~$5 billion, and it raised its 2025 growth capital spending by $875 million to a new range of $3.45 billion-$3.75 billion.

data.title: Jiuzi Holdings to raise $30M in private placement to invest in cryptocurrencies
data.description:
• Jiuzi Holdings (NASDAQ:JZXN) announced a $30M private placement with non-U.S. institutional investors.
  • Funds will primarily be used to purchase cryptocurrencies.
  • Each unit includes one share of common stock at $0.42 and three warrants.
  • Warrants are exercisable at $0.50 per share within three years and may be cashless under certain conditions.
  • JZXN shares up 12.5% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Anavex rises after mid-stage trial data for schizophrenia candidate
data.description:
Anavex Life Sciences (NASDAQ:AVXL) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the New York-based biotech said that a mid-stage trial for its experimental antipsychotic ANAVEX 3-71 reached the primary endpoint in patients with schizophrenia, a psychiatric illness.

Citing topline data, Anavex (NASDAQ:AVXL) announced that ANAVEX 3-71 was found to be safe and well-tolerated in the placebo-controlled Phase 2 clinical study, which enrolled adults with schizophrenia who were undergoing stable antipsychotic medications.

The company added that ANAVEX 3-71 indicated a tolerability profile consistent with prior trials in healthy volunteers, with no serious treatment-emergent adverse events and no severe TEAEs.

Additionally, citing a secondary and exploratory analysis of the trial, the company stated that favorable trends were observed in several outcome measures, including event-related potential biomarkers of schizophrenia.

“The positive safety profile and encouraging biomarker trends support the continued development of ANAVEX 3-71 as a potential treatment for CNS disorders that could address underlying pathophysiology beyond symptomatic control,” CEO Christopher Missling added.

data.title: Dragonfly Energy secures $300K award from Nevada Tech Hub for battery innovation
data.description:
• Dragonfly Energy (NASDAQ:DFLI) was selected by Nevada Tech Hub for ~$300,000 in funding.

  • The award supports modernization and workforce development in lithium battery manufacturing.

  • Funds will help advance ISO 9001 certification and upgrade battery production systems.

  • Project aims to generate six-figure annual savings and reinvest in employee training.

  • Partnerships for workforce development include Truckee Meadows Community College and UNR.

  • The company plans to recruit and upskill production staff in machining, automation, and maintenance.

  • Source: Press release
